#1 From Verdict to Process: Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Multi-Stage Fact Verification
Authors: Rongxin Yang, Shenghong He, Siyuan Zhu, Chao Yu
Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13262v1
Category: cs.AI; Score: 50.7; Updated: 2026-06-11
TL;DR: Recent approaches combining Large Language Models (LLMs) with retrieval-augmented reasoning have shown promise for automated fact verification.
中文摘要: 这篇论文主要围绕 cs.AI 中的 reinforcement learning / post-training 展开。根据摘要，核心问题是：Recent approaches combining Large Language Models (LLMs) with retrieval-augmented reasoning have shown promise for automated fact verification. To process complex claims, these verification pipelines typically execute multi-stage workflows that coordinate tightly coupled modules, including claim decomposition, evidence gathering, and verdict prediction. 适合先作为候选阅读，再结合实验设置判断是否值得精读。
English Summary: Recent approaches combining Large Language Models (LLMs) with retrieval-augmented reasoning have shown promise for automated fact verification. To process complex claims, these verification pipelines typically execute multi-stage workflows that coordinate tightly coupled modules, including claim decomposition, evidence gathering, and verdict prediction. However, existing methods optimize individual stages in isolation or rely on fixed heuristics, which limits adaptive coordination among stages and can lead to suboptimal outcomes.
方法 / Method: reinforcement learning / post-training
贡献 / Contribution: 提供新的评测视角或任务设置，可用于判断模型能力和研究空白。
局限 / Limitation: 可能依赖特定任务集，泛化到真实研究/生产场景仍需进一步验证。
为什么与你相关 / Why relevant: matches large language model, llm, language model, agent；strong interest: inference efficiency；semantically close to your LLM/optimization profile

#1 Fourier Multi-Component and Multi-Layer Neural Networks: Unlocking High-Frequency Potential
Authors: Shijun Zhang, Hongkai Zhao, Yimin Zhong, Haomin Zhou
Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.18959v3
Category: cs.LG; Score: 45.2; Updated: 2026-06-11
TL;DR: The architecture of a neural network and the choice of its activation function are both fundamental to its performance.
中文摘要: 这篇论文主要围绕 cs.LG 中的 inference/training efficiency optimization 展开。根据摘要，核心问题是：The architecture of a neural network and the choice of its activation function are both fundamental to its performance. Equally important is ensuring that these two elements are well matched, as their alignment is key to effective representation and learning. 适合先作为候选阅读，再结合实验设置判断是否值得精读。
English Summary: The architecture of a neural network and the choice of its activation function are both fundamental to its performance. Equally important is ensuring that these two elements are well matched, as their alignment is key to effective representation and learning. In this paper, we introduce the Fourier Multi-Component and Multi-Layer Neural Network (FMMNN), a model that combines sine-type activations with the multi-component and multi-layer structure of MMNNs.
方法 / Method: inference/training efficiency optimization
贡献 / Contribution: 提供领域结构、问题分类或趋势梳理，帮助更新 LLM landscape 认知地图。
局限 / Limitation: 可能依赖特定任务集，泛化到真实研究/生产场景仍需进一步验证。

#1 Distributional Biases in Post-Training: A Markovian Analysis of Reasoning Trajectories
Authors: Dake Bu, Wei Huang, Andi Han, Atsushi Nitanda, Bo Xue, Qingfu Zhang, Hau-San Wong, Taiji Suzuki
Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.07368v3
Category: cs.LG; Score: 58.6; Updated: 2026-06-12
TL;DR: Foundation models exhibit broad knowledge but limited task-specific reasoning, motivating post-training strategies such as RL with verifiable rewards (RLVR) and test-time scaling (TTS).
中文摘要: 这篇论文主要围绕 cs.LG 中的 reinforcement learning / post-training 展开。根据摘要，核心问题是：Foundation models exhibit broad knowledge but limited task-specific reasoning, motivating post-training strategies such as RL with verifiable rewards (RLVR) and test-time scaling (TTS). 适合先作为候选阅读，再结合实验设置判断是否值得精读。
English Summary: Foundation models exhibit broad knowledge but limited task-specific reasoning, motivating post-training strategies such as RL with verifiable rewards (RLVR) and test-time scaling (TTS). While recent work highlights the role of exploration in improving pass@K, empirical evidence points to a paradox: RLVR and ORM/PRM typically reinforce existing paths rather than expanding the reasoning scope, raising the question of why exploration helps if no new patterns emerge. To reconcile this paradox, we adopt the perspective of Kim et al.
方法 / Method: reinforcement learning / post-training
贡献 / Contribution: 提出或分析一种与 LLM/AI 系统相关的方法，具体贡献需结合正文实验确认。
局限 / Limitation: 当前只基于 arXiv 摘要判断，细节、消融和真实效果需要阅读全文确认。
为什么与你相关 / Why relevant: matches foundation model, reasoning, post-training, rlvr；strong interest: test-time scaling, post-training；your recent feedback likes: RLVR

#1 Harmonizing Semantic and Collaborative in LLMs: Reasoning-based Embedding Generator for Sequential Recommendation
Authors: Qidong Liu, Mingyao Huang, Moranxin Wang, Wenxuan Yang, Haiping Zhu
Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16703v1
Category: cs.IR; Score: 59.5; Updated: 2026-06-15
TL;DR: Sequential Recommender Systems (SRS) predict the next item of interest based on users' interaction histories and have been widely deployed, but hindered by long-tail problem.
中文摘要: 这篇论文主要围绕 cs.IR 中的 reinforcement learning / post-training 展开。根据摘要，核心问题是：Sequential Recommender Systems (SRS) predict the next item of interest based on users' interaction histories and have been widely deployed, but hindered by long-tail problem. 适合先作为候选阅读，再结合实验设置判断是否值得精读。
English Summary: Sequential Recommender Systems (SRS) predict the next item of interest based on users' interaction histories and have been widely deployed, but hindered by long-tail problem. Large Language Models (LLMs), with strong semantic understanding and reasoning capabilities, offer a promising way to enrich item semantics and have recently been used as embedding generators. However, two fundamental gaps remain.

#1 Beyond NL2Code: A Structured Survey of Multimodal Code Intelligence
Authors: Xuanle Zhao, Qiushi Sun, Jingyu Xiao, Xuexin Liu, Haoyue Yang, Qiaosheng Chen, Xianzhen Luo, Jing Huang et al.
Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15932v2
Category: cs.CL; Score: 83.0; Updated: 2026-06-16
TL;DR: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have substantially advanced text-to-code synthesis, many real programming tasks specify intent through visual artifacts such as screenshots, charts, vector drawings, videos, and interactive states.
中文摘要: 这篇论文主要围绕 cs.CL 中的 agent or tool-use evaluation 展开。根据摘要，核心问题是：While Large Language Models (LLMs) have substantially advanced text-to-code synthesis, many real programming tasks specify intent through visual artifacts such as screenshots, charts, vector drawings, videos, and interactive states. 适合先作为候选阅读，再结合实验设置判断是否值得精读。
English Summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have substantially advanced text-to-code synthesis, many real programming tasks specify intent through visual artifacts such as screenshots, charts, vector drawings, videos, and interactive states. These tasks require models to connect visual perception to executable programs, because correctness depends not only on syntax but also on layout, data semantics, interaction behavior, and domain-specific constraints that apply after execution. This survey examines Multimodal Code Intelligence, covering systems that generate, edit, refine, or reason with code under visually grounded inputs and outputs.
方法 / Method: agent or tool-use evaluation
贡献 / Contribution: 提供领域结构、问题分类或趋势梳理，帮助更新 LLM landscape 认知地图。
局限 / Limitation: 可能依赖特定任务集，泛化到真实研究/生产场景仍需进一步验证。

#1 REVES: REvision and VErification--Augmented Training for Test-Time Scaling
Authors: Yuanxin Liu, Ruida Zhou, Xinyan Zhao, Amr Sharaf, Hongzhou Lin, Arijit Biswas, Mohammad Ghavamzadeh, Zhaoran Wang et al.
Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18910v1
Category: cs.LG; Score: 77.9; Updated: 2026-06-17
TL;DR: Test-time scaling via sequential revision has emerged as a powerful paradigm for enhancing Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning.
中文摘要: 这篇论文主要围绕 cs.LG 中的 reinforcement learning / post-training 展开。根据摘要，核心问题是：Test-time scaling via sequential revision has emerged as a powerful paradigm for enhancing Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning. However, standard post-training methods primarily optimize single-shot objectives, creating a fundamental misalignment with multi-step inference dynamics. 适合先作为候选阅读，再结合实验设置判断是否值得精读。
English Summary: Test-time scaling via sequential revision has emerged as a powerful paradigm for enhancing Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning. However, standard post-training methods primarily optimize single-shot objectives, creating a fundamental misalignment with multi-step inference dynamics. While recent work treats this as multi-turn reinforcement learning (RL), conventional approaches optimize over the multi-step trajectories directly, failing to further exploit the high-quality mistakes in intermediate steps that model can learn from correcting them.
方法 / Method: reinforcement learning / post-training
贡献 / Contribution: 尝试改善训练、推理或服务效率，对 LLM optimization 方向有参考价值。
局限 / Limitation: 效率收益可能受模型规模、硬件、batch 设置和实现细节影响，需关注实验配置。
为什么与你相关 / Why relevant: matches large language model, llm, language model, reasoning；strong interest: test-time scaling, post-training；your recent feedback likes: alignment

#1 Beyond Reasoning Gains: Mitigating General-Capability Forgetting in Large Reasoning Models
Authors: Hoang Phan, Xianjun Yang, Yuanshun Yao, Jingyu Zhang, Shengjie Bi, Xiaocheng Tang, Madian Khabsa, Lijuan Liu et al.
Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.21978v2
Category: cs.LG; Score: 87.0; Updated: 2026-06-18
TL;DR: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has delivered impressive gains in mathematical and multimodal reasoning and has become a standard post-training paradigm for contemporary language and vision-language models.
中文摘要: 这篇论文主要围绕 cs.LG 中的 reinforcement learning / post-training 展开。根据摘要，核心问题是：Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has delivered impressive gains in mathematical and multimodal reasoning and has become a standard post-training paradigm for contemporary language and vision-language models. 适合先作为候选阅读，再结合实验设置判断是否值得精读。
English Summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has delivered impressive gains in mathematical and multimodal reasoning and has become a standard post-training paradigm for contemporary language and vision-language models. However, the RLVR recipe introduces a significant risk of capability regression, in which models forget foundational skills after prolonged training without employing regularization strategies. We empirically confirm this concern, observing that open-source reasoning models suffer performance degradation on core capabilities such as perception and faithfulness.
方法 / Method: reinforcement learning / post-training
贡献 / Contribution: 提供新的评测视角或任务设置，可用于判断模型能力和研究空白。
局限 / Limitation: 可能依赖特定任务集，泛化到真实研究/生产场景仍需进一步验证。
为什么与你相关 / Why relevant: matches language model, reasoning, post-training, rlvr；strong interest: post-training, reasoning model；your recent feedback likes: RLVR
